1â†’---
     2â†’description: Execute full task workflow through BA â†’ Developer â†’ Tester â†’ Documenter
     3â†’allowed-tools: Task, AskUserQuestion, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash
     4â†’argument-hint: <task-description>
     5â†’---
     6â†’
     7â†’# Task Workflow Command
     8â†’
     9â†’Execute complete task workflow for: $ARGUMENTS
    10â†’
    11â†’## Workflow Overview
    12â†’
    13â†’```
    14â†’BA (questions?) â†’ Developer (questions?) â†’ Tester (bugs? â†’ fix â†’ retest) â†’ Documenter â†’ Report
    15â†’```
    16â†’
    17â†’---
    18â†’
    19â†’## Step 1: Initialize Task
    20â†’
    21â†’Generate unique task ID: `task-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`
    22â†’
    23â†’Create task folder: `tasks/{task-id}/`
    24â†’
    25â†’---
    26â†’
    27â†’## Step 2: Business Analyst Phase
    28â†’
    29â†’### Phase 1 - Research & Questions
    30â†’
    31â†’Call the `business-analyst` agent:
    32â†’
    33â†’```
    34â†’Task ID: {task-id}
    35â†’Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"
    36â†’
    37â†’Execute Phase 1: Research & Questions
    38â†’
    39â†’1. Read `.claude/docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md` to understand existing business logic
    40â†’2. Identify what's unclear about this requirement
    41â†’3. Return clarifying questions in this format:
    42â†’
    43â†’QUESTIONS:
    44â†’1. [question]
    45â†’   - Option A: [description]
    46â†’   - Option B: [description]
    47â†’
    48â†’2. [question]
    49â†’   - Option A: [description]
    50â†’   - Option B: [description]
    51â†’
    52â†’Do NOT write any files yet. Only return questions.
    53â†’If everything is clear, respond with: NO_QUESTIONS
    54â†’```
    55â†’
    56â†’### Handle Questions
    57â†’
    58â†’If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:
    59â†’
    60â†’1. Use `AskUserQuestion` tool to present questions to user
    61â†’2. Collect answers
    62â†’3. Proceed to Phase 2 with answers
    63â†’
    64â†’If agent returns `NO_QUESTIONS`:
    65â†’
    66â†’1. Proceed to Phase 2 without answers
    67â†’
    68â†’### Phase 2 - Write Requirements
    69â†’
    70â†’Call the `business-analyst` agent:
    71â†’
    72â†’```
    73â†’Task ID: {task-id}
    74â†’Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"
    75â†’
    76â†’Execute Phase 2: Write Requirements
    77â†’
    78â†’User answers:
    79â†’{answers from Phase 1, or "No clarification needed" if NO_QUESTIONS}
    80â†’
    81â†’Write business requirements to: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
    82â†’Follow output format from your instructions.
    83â†’```
    84â†’
    85â†’**Check status:**
    86â†’
    87â†’- `Status: Done` â†’ proceed to Developer
    88â†’- `Status: Failed` â†’ stop and report error
    89â†’- `Status: Needs Review` â†’ report to user and proceed
    90â†’
    91â†’---
    92â†’
    93â†’## Step 3: Developer Phase
    94â†’
    95â†’Call the `developer` agent:
    96â†’
    97â†’```
    98â†’Task ID: {task-id}
    99â†’
   100â†’Read: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
   101â†’
   102â†’Design the optimal technical solution and implement the code.
   103â†’Follow your instructions for architecture decisions and code standards.
   104â†’
   105â†’If you need clarification about requirements, return:
   106â†’QUESTIONS:
   107â†’1. [question about requirement]
   108â†’2. [question about approach]
   109â†’
   110â†’Otherwise, implement and write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
   111â†’```
   112â†’
   113â†’### Handle Developer Questions
   114â†’
   115â†’If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:
   116â†’
   117â†’1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
   118â†’2. Call developer again with answers:
   119â†’
   120â†’```
   121â†’Task ID: {task-id}
   122â†’Continuing implementation.
   123â†’
   124â†’User answers:
   125â†’{user answers}
   126â†’
   127â†’Complete implementation and write to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
   128â†’```
   129â†’
   130â†’**Check status:**
   131â†’
   132â†’- `Status: Done` â†’ proceed to Tester
   133â†’- `Status: Failed` â†’ stop and report error
   134â†’
   135â†’---
   136â†’
   137â†’## Step 4: Tester Phase
   138â†’
   139â†’Call the `tester` agent:
   140â†’
   141â†’```
   142â†’Task ID: {task-id}
   143â†’
   144â†’Read these files:
   145â†’- tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
   146â†’- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
   147â†’
   148â†’Execute full testing:
   149â†’1. Code review
   150â†’2. Write unit tests (mandatory for services/utils)
   151â†’3. Write E2E tests (if critical flow)
   152â†’4. Run tests
   153â†’5. Find and report bugs
   154â†’
   155â†’Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
   156â†’```
   157â†’
   158â†’### Handle Bugs
   159â†’
   160â†’Parse tester output for bugs. If `## Bugs Found` section contains bugs:
   161â†’
   162â†’**Bug Fix Iteration 1:**
   163â†’
   164â†’1. Call `developer` agent to fix:
   165â†’
   166â†’```
   167â†’Task ID: {task-id}
   168â†’
   169â†’BUGS TO FIX:
   170â†’{list of bugs from tester output}
   171â†’
   172â†’Read: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
   173â†’
   174â†’Fix all listed bugs.
   175â†’Update your implementation.
   176â†’Update: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md with fixes.
   177â†’```
   178â†’
   179â†’2. Call `tester` agent to retest:
   180â†’
   181â†’```
   182â†’Task ID: {task-id}
   183â†’
   184â†’RETEST after bug fixes.
   185â†’
   186â†’Read:
   187â†’- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md (updated)
   188â†’- Previous bugs that were fixed
   189â†’
   190â†’Verify fixes and check for new issues.
   191â†’Update: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
   192â†’```
   193â†’
   194â†’**After Retest:**
   195â†’
   196â†’- No bugs â†’ proceed to Documenter
   197â†’- Still bugs â†’ proceed to Documenter, but note bugs in final report
   198â†’
   199â†’---
   200â†’
   201â†’## Step 5: Documenter Phase
   202â†’
   203â†’Call the `documenter` agent:
   204â†’
   205â†’```
   206â†’Task ID: {task-id}
   207â†’
   208â†’Read all outputs in tasks/{task-id}/:
   209â†’- business-analyst-output.md
   210â†’- developer-output.md
   211â†’- tester-output.md
   212â†’
   213â†’Update documentation in .claude/docs/ based on changes.
   214â†’Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md
   215â†’```
   216â†’
   217â†’---
   218â†’
   219â†’## Step 6: Format & Report
   220â†’
   221â†’### Format All Files
   222â†’
   223â†’```bash
   224â†’npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
   225â†’```
   226â†’
   227â†’### Write Command Report
   228â†’
   229â†’Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`
   230â†’
   231â†’```markdown
   232â†’# Command Report: {task-id}
   233â†’
   234â†’## Task
   235â†’
   236â†’[Brief task description]
   237â†’
   238â†’## Agent Sequence
   239â†’
   240â†’1. Business Analyst: {status}
   241â†’2. Developer: {status}
   242â†’3. Tester: {status}
   243â†’4. Documenter: {status}
   244â†’
   245â†’## Summary
   246â†’
   247â†’- BA: [key points from requirements]
   248â†’- Developer: [files changed]
   249â†’- Tester: [test results]
   250â†’- Documenter: [docs updated]
   251â†’
   252â†’## Issues
   253â†’
   254â†’[Command failures, timeouts, or agent errors if any]
   255â†’
   256â†’## Status
   257â†’
   258â†’{COMPLETED / FAILED}
   259â†’```
   260â†’
   261â†’### Console Output
   262â†’
   263â†’Print only the final status to console:
   264â†’
   265â†’```
   266â†’TASK {task-id}: COMPLETED
   267â†’```
   268â†’
   269â†’or
   270â†’
   271â†’```
   272â†’TASK {task-id}: FAILED
   273â†’```
   274â†’
   275â†’Do NOT print detailed information to console. All details go in markdown files.
   276â†’
   277â†’---
   278â†’
   279â†’## Error Handling
   280â†’
   281â†’### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`
   282â†’
   283â†’Stop workflow immediately. Report:
   284â†’
   285â†’```
   286â†’Task failed at {agent-name} phase.
   287â†’Reason: {reason from status}
   288â†’Partial outputs saved to: tasks/{task-id}/
   289â†’```
   290â†’
   291â†’### Agent Returns Questions
   292â†’
   293â†’Always use `AskUserQuestion` to get user input. Never guess or assume.
   294â†’
   295â†’### Tester Finds Bugs After Retry
   296â†’
   297â†’Complete the workflow but include bugs in final report:
   298â†’
   299â†’```
   300â†’## Known Issues
   301â†’
   302â†’The following bugs were found and could not be fixed in one iteration:
   303â†’
   304â†’{bug list}
   305â†’
   306â†’Manual review required.
   307â†’```
   308â†’
   309â†’---
   310â†’
   311â†’## Important Rules
   312â†’
   313â†’1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
   314â†’2. **Always ask questions** - Never skip user clarification when agent requests it
   315â†’3. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain after one fix cycle, complete with report
   316â†’4. **Run prettier** - Format all markdown files after completion
   317â†’5. **Report everything** - Include all statuses and issues in final report
   318â†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
---
description: Execute full task workflow through BA â†’ Developer â†’ Tester â†’ Documenter
allowed-tools: Task, AskUserQuestion, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash
argument-hint: <task-description>
---

# Task Workflow Command

Execute complete task workflow for: $ARGUMENTS

## Workflow Overview

```
BA (questions?) â†’ Developer (questions?) â†’ Tester (bugs? â†’ fix â†’ retest) â†’ Documenter â†’ Report
```

---

## Step 1: Initialize Task

Generate unique task ID: `task-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`

Create task folder: `tasks/{task-id}/`

---

## Step 2: Business Analyst Phase

### Phase 1 - Research & Questions

Call the `business-analyst` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

Execute Phase 1: Research & Questions

1. Read `.claude/docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md` to understand existing business logic
2. Identify what's unclear about this requirement
3. Return clarifying questions in this format:

QUESTIONS:
1. [question]
   - Option A: [description]
   - Option B: [description]

2. [question]
   - Option A: [description]
   - Option B: [description]

Do NOT write any files yet. Only return questions.
If everything is clear, respond with: NO_QUESTIONS
```

### Handle Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` tool to present questions to user
2. Collect answers
3. Proceed to Phase 2 with answers

If agent returns `NO_QUESTIONS`:

1. Proceed to Phase 2 without answers

### Phase 2 - Write Requirements

Call the `business-analyst` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

Execute Phase 2: Write Requirements

User answers:
{answers from Phase 1, or "No clarification needed" if NO_QUESTIONS}

Write business requirements to: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
Follow output format from your instructions.
```

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` â†’ proceed to Developer
- `Status: Failed` â†’ stop and report error
- `Status: Needs Review` â†’ report to user and proceed

---

## Step 3: Developer Phase

Call the `developer` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md

Design the optimal technical solution and implement the code.
Follow your instructions for architecture decisions and code standards.

If you need clarification about requirements, return:
QUESTIONS:
1. [question about requirement]
2. [question about approach]

Otherwise, implement and write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

### Handle Developer Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
2. Call developer again with answers:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Continuing implementation.

User answers:
{user answers}

Complete implementation and write to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` â†’ proceed to Tester
- `Status: Failed` â†’ stop and report error

---

## Step 4: Tester Phase

Call the `tester` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read these files:
- tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

Execute full testing:
1. Code review
2. Write unit tests (mandatory for services/utils)
3. Write E2E tests (if critical flow)
4. Run tests
5. Find and report bugs

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

### Handle Bugs

Parse tester output for bugs. If `## Bugs Found` section contains bugs:

**Bug Fix Iteration 1:**

1. Call `developer` agent to fix:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

BUGS TO FIX:
{list of bugs from tester output}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md

Fix all listed bugs.
Update your implementation.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md with fixes.
```

2. Call `tester` agent to retest:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

RETEST after bug fixes.

Read:
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md (updated)
- Previous bugs that were fixed

Verify fixes and check for new issues.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

**After Retest:**

- No bugs â†’ proceed to Documenter
- Still bugs â†’ proceed to Documenter, but note bugs in final report

---

## Step 5: Documenter Phase

Call the `documenter` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read all outputs in tasks/{task-id}/:
- business-analyst-output.md
- developer-output.md
- tester-output.md

Update documentation in .claude/docs/ based on changes.
Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md
```

---

## Step 6: Format & Report

### Format All Files

```bash
npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
```

### Write Command Report

Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`

```markdown
# Command Report: {task-id}

## Task

[Brief task description]

## Agent Sequence

1. Business Analyst: {status}
2. Developer: {status}
3. Tester: {status}
4. Documenter: {status}

## Summary

- BA: [key points from requirements]
- Developer: [files changed]
- Tester: [test results]
- Documenter: [docs updated]

## Issues

[Command failures, timeouts, or agent errors if any]

## Status

{COMPLETED / FAILED}
```

### Console Output

Print only the final status to console:

```
TASK {task-id}: COMPLETED
```

or

```
TASK {task-id}: FAILED
```

Do NOT print detailed information to console. All details go in markdown files.

---

## Error Handling

### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`

Stop workflow immediately. Report:

```
Task failed at {agent-name} phase.
Reason: {reason from status}
Partial outputs saved to: tasks/{task-id}/
```

### Agent Returns Questions

Always use `AskUserQuestion` to get user input. Never guess or assume.

### Tester Finds Bugs After Retry

Complete the workflow but include bugs in final report:

```
## Known Issues

The following bugs were found and could not be fixed in one iteration:

{bug list}

Manual review required.
```

---

## Important Rules

1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
2. **Always ask questions** - Never skip user clarification when agent requests it
3. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain after one fix cycle, complete with report
4. **Run prettier** - Format all markdown files after completion
5. **Report everything** - Include all statuses and issues in final report

Web search results for query: "Claude Code agent-check agent:improve built-in agents 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"Subagents - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/sub-agents"},{"title":"Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices"},{"title":"Enabling Claude Code to work more autonomously","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/enabling-claude-code-to-work-more-autonomously"},{"title":"AI agents | Claude","url":"https://claude.com/solutions/agents"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Agent in JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains AI Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2025/09/introducing-claude-agent-in-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills"},{"title":"How I Turned Claude Code Into My Personal AI Agent Operating System for Writing and Research","url":"https://aimaker.substack.com/p/how-i-turned-claude-code-into-personal-ai-agent-operating-system-for-writing-research-complete-guide"},{"title":"How to turn Claude Code into a domain specific coding agent","url":"https://blog.langchain.com/how-to-turn-claude-code-into-a-domain-specific-coding-agent/"},{"title":"ClaudeLog - Claude Code Docs, Guides, Tutorials & Best Practices","url":"https://claudelog.com/mechanics/agent-engineering/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code's agent capabilities and built-in agents:

## Claude Code Built-in Agents and Subagents

Claude Code includes built-in subagents that are available out of the box: The general-purpose subagent is a capable agent for complex, multi-step tasks that require both exploration and action.(no content)Unlike the Explore subagent, it can modify files and execute a wider range of operations.

### Checking and Improving Agent Output

"Agents that can check and improve their own output are fundamentally more reliableâ€”they catch mistakes before they compound, self-correct when they drift, and get better as they iterate."(no content)The key is giving Claude concrete ways to evaluate its work. There are three approaches found to be effective: the best form of feedback is providing clearly defined rules for an output, then explaining which rules failed and why.

### Managing Custom Agents

To create and use a subagent immediately without restarting, use the /agents command instead.(no content)The /agents command provides an interactive interface that lists all available tools, including any connected MCP server tools, making it easier to select the ones you need.

### Best Practices for Custom Agents

Design focused subagents with single, clear responsibilities rather than trying to make one subagent do everything. This improves performance and makes subagents more predictable.(no content)To encourage more proactive subagent use, include phrases like "use PROACTIVELY" or "MUST BE USED" in your description field.

### Claude Agent SDK (formerly Claude Code SDK)

The agent harness that powers Claude Code (the Claude Code SDK) can power many other types of agents. To reflect this broader vision, Anthropic renamed the Claude Code SDK to the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)For teams who want to create custom agentic experiences, the Claude Agent SDK gives access to the same core tools, context management systems, and permissions frameworks that power Claude Code.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills"},{"title":"ClaudeLog - Claude Code Docs, Guides, Tutorials & Best Practices","url":"https://claudelog.com/mechanics/agent-engineering/"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Agent in JetBrains IDEs | The JetBrains AI Blog","url":"https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2025/09/introducing-claude-agent-in-jetbrains-ides/"},{"title":"Enabling Claude Code to work more autonomously","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/enabling-claude-code-to-work-more-autonomously"},{"title":"GitHub - wshobson/agents: Intelligent automation and multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code","url":"https://github.com/wshobson/agents"},{"title":"AI agents | Claude","url":"https://claude.com/solutions/agents"},{"title":"Optimizing Agentic Coding: How to use Claude Code","url":"https://research.aimultiple.com/agentic-coding/"},{"title":"Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices"},{"title":"How I Turned Claude Code Into My Personal AI Agent Operating System for Writing and Research","url":"https://aimaker.substack.com/p/how-i-turned-claude-code-into-personal-ai-agent-operating-system-for-writing-research-complete-guide"}]

Here are the search results for "Claude Code agent:improve system agent 2025":

## Key Findings

### Improving Claude Code Agents

The best way to improve an agent is to look carefully at its output, especially the cases where it fails, and to put yourself in its shoes: does it have the right tools for the job? If your agent misunderstands the task, it might be missing key information.

### Key Questions for Agent Improvement

According to Anthropic's guide on building agents with the Claude Agent SDK:
- Can you alter the structure of your search APIs to make it easier to find what it needs to know?
- If your agent fails at a task repeatedly, can you add a formal rule in your tool calls to identify and fix the failure?
- If your agent can't fix its errors, can you give it more useful or creative tools to approach the problem differently?
- If your agent's performance varies as you add features, build a representative test set for programmatic evaluations (or evals) based on customer usage.

### Self-Improving Agents

Agents that can check and improve their own output are fundamentally more reliableâ€”they catch mistakes before they compound, self-correct when they drift, and get better as they iterate.

### Agent Skills (October 2025)

Anthropic created Agent Skills: organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that agents can discover and load dynamically to perform better at specific tasks. Skills extend Claude's capabilities by packaging your expertise into composable resources for Claude, transforming general-purpose agents into specialized agents that fit your needs.

### Claude Agent SDK

The agent harness that powers Claude Code (the Claude Code SDK) can power many other types of agents. To reflect this broader vision, Anthropic renamed the Claude Code SDK to the Claude Agent SDK.(no content)The Claude Agent SDK gives access to the same core tools, context management systems, and permissions frameworks that power Claude Code. Anthropic has also released SDK support for subagents and hooks, making it more customizable for building agents for specific workflows.

### Agent Engineering Best Practices

For custom agents: Create focused, single-purpose agents initially. Start with lightweight agents (minimal or no tools) for maximum composability. Carefully engineer how many tokens your custom agent needs to initialize. Prioritize efficiency over capability for frequent-use agents.

### JetBrains IDE Integration

The Claude Agent is now seamlessly integrated into JetBrains IDEs via the AI chat and included in the JetBrains AI subscription. No extra plugins, no extra subscriptions â€“ just advanced AI coding assistance built on Anthropic's Agent SDK, which powers Claude Code, right inside your IDE.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

**Severity:** Low | Medium | High
**Description:** {what to improve}
**Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
```

content.text: Let me check if "agent:improve" might be a reference to the agent-lint instructions themselves by looking at your prompt context more carefully. Let me also check for any similar patterns:

# Validation Report: agent:improve

**Type:** Agent (System - suspected)
**File:** Not found
**Status:** FAIL

## Summary

The "agent:improve" agent does not exist as a file in the project or global configuration directories. This appears to be either a system agent reference that doesn't exist, a typo, or a conceptual agent that has not been implemented. No file-based definition was found in `.claude/agents/`, `.claude/commands/`, `~/.claude/agents/`, or any related directories.

## Checks

| Check                | Status | Notes                                                    |
| -------------------- | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Description Clarity  | FAIL   | No definition found - cannot evaluate                    |
| Responsibility Scope | FAIL   | No definition found - cannot evaluate                    |
| Conflict Detection   | FAIL   | Cannot check conflicts without definition                |
| Redundancy           | FAIL   | Cannot check redundancy without definition               |
| Tool Access          | N/A    | No definition found                                      |
| Output Format        | N/A    | No definition found                                      |
| Instructions Quality | FAIL   | No definition found - cannot evaluate                    |

## Issues Found

### Issue 1: Agent Definition Not Found

**Severity:** Critical
**Location:** N/A
**Problem:** No file or definition exists for "agent:improve" in any of the following locations:
- `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/agents/`
- `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/.claude/commands/`
- `/Users/dmitry/.claude/agents/` (global)
- No matches found in any `.claude/` directories

The search revealed the following existing agents in the project:
- `business-analyst.md`
- `developer.md`
- `tester.md`
- `documenter.md`

And one command:
- `task:create.md`

**Suggestion:** 
1. Verify the agent name - it may be a typo. Did you mean one of the existing agents above?
2. If this agent should exist, create it with a clear definition following the pattern of existing agents
3. If this was meant to reference a system agent like `agent-check`, use the correct system agent name

### Issue 2: Not a Known System Agent

**Severity:** High
**Location:** N/A
**Problem:** Based on web research and documentation, "agent:improve" is not a recognized built-in system agent in Claude Code. Known system agents include:
- `general-purpose` (subagent for complex tasks)
- `Explore` (for exploration tasks)
- `Plan` (for planning)
- `agent-check` (for validation)
- `claude-code-guide` (for documentation)
- `statusline-setup` (for configuration)

**Suggestion:** If you intended to validate the agent validation system itself (the `agent-lint` agent), that is a system agent built into this conversation context and cannot be validated as a file.

## Conflicts Detected

Cannot detect conflicts without a valid agent definition.

## Improvement Suggestions

### If Creating "agent:improve" as a New Agent

Based on best practices from research, if you intend to create an agent that helps improve code or agents:

1. **Define Clear Scope** - Decide what "improve" means:
   - Code quality improvement?
   - Agent definition improvement?
   - Performance optimization?
   - Architecture refinement?

2. **Follow Single Responsibility Principle** - Create focused agents:
   - Instead of one "improve" agent, consider specific agents like:
     - `code-reviewer` (for code quality)
     - `agent-validator` (for agent definitions)
     - `performance-optimizer` (for performance)

3. **Avoid Overlapping with Existing Agents**:
   - The `tester` agent already handles code quality and improvements
   - The `developer` agent handles implementation improvements
   - Any new agent should have a distinct, non-overlapping purpose

4. **Use Clear Naming Convention**:
   - If creating a command, use format: `{action}:{target}` (e.g., `improve:code`)
   - If creating an agent, use descriptive nouns (e.g., `code-reviewer`, not `improver`)

## Research Findings

From web research on Claude Code agent best practices (December 2025):

### Key Principles for Agent Improvement

1. **Self-Checking Agents are More Reliable** - "Agents that can check and improve their own output are fundamentally more reliableâ€”they catch mistakes before they compound, self-correct when they drift, and get better as they iterate."

2. **Focus and Single Responsibility** - "Design focused subagents with single, clear responsibilities rather than trying to make one subagent do everything. This improves performance and makes subagents more predictable."

3. **Agent Skills (New in 2025)** - Anthropic introduced Agent Skills: organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that agents can discover and load dynamically to perform better at specific tasks.

4. **Evaluation Questions** for improving agents:
   - Does it have the right tools for the job?
   - If it misunderstands tasks, is it missing key information?
   - Can you add formal rules to identify and fix repeated failures?
   - If it can't fix errors, can you give it more useful or creative tools?

5. **Testing and Iteration** - Build representative test sets for programmatic evaluations based on actual usage patterns.

### Recommendations Based on Research

If creating an improvement-focused agent:
- Make it **self-evaluating** with concrete rules for output quality
- Give it **specific, measurable criteria** for what constitutes "improved"
- Provide **examples of good vs bad** outputs
- Include **anti-patterns** to avoid
- Ensure it has **appropriate tools** for its scope

## Suggestions Log

Created initial entry for non-existent agent "agent:improve".

### Suggestion: Create Agent Definition or Clarify Intent
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The agent "agent:improve" was requested for validation but does not exist. Either create the agent definition file or clarify the actual agent name to validate.
**Status:** Pending

---

## Conclusion

**Cannot validate "agent:improve"** - no definition exists. Please:

1. Confirm the correct agent name you want to validate
2. If creating a new agent, follow the patterns in existing agents and the best practices outlined above
3. If validating an existing agent, use one of: `business-analyst`, `developer`, `tester`, or `documenter`

Sources:
- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- [Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
- [Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills)
